head_commit.message:
fix(riscv): set FS before fp restore/clear in switch_to (#30)

Move sstatus::set_fs(next_fp_state.fs) before the restore/clear match
so FP instructions are executable when firmware leaves FS=Off.

run.display_title: fix(riscv): set FS before fp restore/clear in switch_to (#30)

head_commit.message:
feat(arm): add ARM architecture support with context and trap handling (#16)

* feat(arm): add ARM architecture support with context and trap handling

* Handling debug events in prefetch abort exceptions, allowing execution to proceed to the next instruction when encountering a breakpoint.

* add read/write_thread_pointer

* add uspace for arceos clippy

* update percpu to 0.3

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

head_commit.message:
fix(riscv): avoid IllegalInstruction when FS=Off during fp switch

On some boot paths/firmware, sstatus.FS may remain Off before task
switch.
In that state, fp-simd restore/clear can execute FP instructions and
trap
with IllegalInstruction.
Fix this in the fp context switch path by explicitly enabling FP
execution
before restore/clear, then keep existing behavior of setting FS to
next_fp_state.fs after the switch.
This keeps the fix in axcpu (root cause location) and avoids relying on
OS-specific early init ordering.

run.display_title: Copilot code review
